title: Heuchera sichotensis
taxonomy: Heuchera sichotensis, also known as Sichotan alumroot, is a species of flowering plant in the saxifrage family, Saxifragaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia. The plant is a perennial that produces small white flowers in late spring and has maple-like leaves in shades of silver, gold, and burgundy. Heuchera sichotensis is a member of the genus Heuchera, which comprises about 40 species of flowering plants. The plant is often grown as an ornamental in gardens for its unique foliage and low-maintenance requirements.
